name: Pfaff, Jean Marie
bio: Pfaff was named goalkeeper of the 1986 FIFA World Cup, and he was the first player to receive the IFFHS World's Best Goalkeeper award in 1987.

name: Schmeichel, Peter
bio: Regarded as one of the greatest goalkeepers of all time, he was voted the IFFHS World's Best Goalkeeper in 1992 and 1993, while the International Federation of Football History & Statistics ranked Schmeichel among the top 10 goalkeepers of the 20th century.

name: Kahn, Oliver
bio: Kahn is one of the most successful German players in recent history

name: Barthez, Fabien
bio: French football (soccer) player, who won honours as a goalkeeper with Marseille, Manchester United and the French national team,

name: Buffon, Gianluigi
bio: He is widely considered by experts to be one of the most dominant and successful goalkeepers in history.

name: Casillas, Iker
bio: Spanish football goalkeeper popularly dubbed "San Iker" ("Saint Iker") for his ability to produce spectacular saves.

name: Courtois, Thibaut
bio: Regarded as one of the greatest goalkeepers of all time, he is known for his reflexes, acrobatic saves, and command of the penalty area.

name: Zoff, Dino
bio: Italian football goalie and the oldest ever winner of the World Cup won in 1982 when Zoff was 40.

name: Maier, Sepp
bio: German footballer and team manager, a goal keeper for Bayern Munich from 1962-1979, and with the West German national team during the same period.

name: Carbajal, Antonio
bio: Mexican soccer goalkeeper nicknamed "Tota" and also called "El Cinco Copas" in reference to his record of five World Cups played.

name: Peruzzi, Angelo
bio: Italian football coach and goalkeeper who is regarded by pundits as one of the greatest Italian goalkeepers of all time, and as one of the best goalkeepers of his generation. He is a three-time winner of the Serie A Goalkeeper of the Year award.

name: Pagliuca, Gianluca
bio: Italian soccer/football player, a goalkeeper who has played for Sampdoria (1987–1994), Internazionale (1994–1999), Bologna (1999–2006), and Ascoli (2006–2007). He also played for the Italian national team from 1991 to 1998.

name: Abbiati, Christian
bio: Italian goalkeeper whose honours include three Serie A titles, one Coppa Italia, two Supercoppa Italiana victories, one UEFA Champions League, and one UEFA Super Cup.

name: Toldo, Francesco
bio: Italian goalkeeper who is regarded by pundits as one of the greatest goalkeepers of his generation.

name: Frey, Sébastien
bio: French footballer who plays as a goalkeeper for Bursaspor in the Turkish Süper Lig.

name: Pelizzoli, Ivan
bio: Italian former professional soccer player who was a goalkeeper. He was one of three over-age players representing Italy at the 2004 Summer Olympics and won a bronze medal.

name: Lloris, Hugo
bio: French football/soccer player, a goalkeeper for English club Tottenham Hotspur and captain of the France national team.

name: Domingo, Marcel
bio: French soccer player.

name: Santiago, Jordan
bio: Spanish-Canadian soccer player and coach who last played for Dutch club SC Veendam, as a goalkeeper.

name: Rost, Frank
bio: German professional footballer who played as a goalkeeper.

name: Mayorga, Roger
bio: Nicaraguan soccer player, regarded as the finest of Nicaraguan goalkeepers alongside Salvador Dubois.

name: Ferretti, Emerson
bio: Brazilian soccer goalkeeper who played at senior club level from 1991 to 2007 after competing as a Brazil youth international.

name: Bernard, Pierre
bio: French soccer player, a goalkeeper who made 21 appearances for the France national team.

name: Ettori, Jean-Luc
bio: French footballer, a goalkeeper who spent his entire career with AS Monaco, and had held the record for the most appearances by any player in Ligue 1 or Division 1 with 602 appearances until 4 December 2013 when Mickaël Landreau surpassed his record.

name: Landreau, Mickaël
bio: French football/soccer goalkeeper, who has 11 caps for the French national team. Landreau has made the most top-flight appearances of any player in the French league.

name: Mancini, Francesco
bio: Italian soccer team coach and player, a goalkeeper who started his career at hometown club Matera in 1985.

name: Taglialatela, Giuseppe
bio: Italian footballer who played as a goalkeeper. He earned the nicknames "pipistrello" (bat), and "Batman," due to his consistent performances, reactions, athleticism, and agility.

name: Cappone, Lionel
bio: French football/soccer player, a goalkeeper playing at senior level since 1995.

name: Gill, Thomas
bio: Norwegian footballer and coach who has been a goalkeeper for clubs such as Brann, Start, Vålerenga, Aalborg, Sturm Graz, and Duisburg. He played five games for the Norwegian national team.

name: Konsel, Michael
bio: Retired Austrian football goalkeeper.

name: Kopa, Raymond
bio: French soccer player.

name: Sivori, Omar
bio: Argentine-Italian soccer player and manager.

name: Law, Denis
bio: Law spent 11 years at Manchester United, where he scored 237 goals in 404 appearances.

name: Rivera, Gianni
bio: Italian politician and former footballer who played as a midfielder.

name: Müller, Gerd
bio: German footballer, a prolific striker renowned for his clinical finishing, especially in and around the six-yard box, he is widely regarded as one of the greatest goalscorers in the history of the sport.

name: Cruyff, Johan
bio: Dutch professional football player and manager. Regarded as one of the greatest players in history and as the greatest Dutch footballer ever, he won the Ballon d'Or three times, in 1971, 1973, and 1974.

name: Beckenbauer, Franz
bio: German football coach, manager, and former player, nicknamed Der Kaiser ("The Emperor") because of his elegant style, his leadership, his first name "Franz" (reminiscent of the Austrian emperors), and his dominance on the football pitch.

name: Rossi, Paolo
bio: Italian soccer forward who led Italy to the 1982 FIFA World Cup title, scoring six goals to win the Golden Boot as top goalscorer, and the Golden Ball for the player of the tournament.

name: Platini, Michel
bio: French soccer player, trainer and manager.

name: Gullit, Ruud
bio: Dutch former footballer and subsequent manager. He was noted for his ability to play in multiple positions.

name: Papin, Jean-Pierre
bio: French football manager and player who played as a forward.

name: Baggio, Roberto
bio: Italian former professional footballer who mainly played as a second striker, or as an attacking midfielder, although he was capable of playing in several offensive positions.

name: Weah, George
bio: Liberian politician and professional footballer who served as the 25th president of Liberia from 2018 to 2024.

name: Zidane, Zinedine
bio: French footballer who is widely regarded as one of the best players of all time, now retired.

name: Rivaldo
bio: Brazilian soccer star who played mainly as an attacking midfielder but also as a second striker, and on occasion deployed as a wide midfielder or as a winger.

name: Figo, Luís
bio: Portuguese football winger who played for the clubs Sporting CP (1989–1995), Barcelona (1995-2000), Real Madrid (2000–2005) and Inter Milan (2005-2009).

name: Nedvěd, Pavel
bio: Czech footballer who played as a midfielder. He is widely considered as one of the greatest Czech players of all time.

name: Shevchenko, Andriy
bio: Retired footballer who played for Dynamo Kyiv, Milan, Chelsea and the Ukraine national team as a striker.

name: Ronaldinho
city: Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), Brazil
bio: Brazilian football/soccer player, also known as Ronaldinho Gaúcho.

name: Cannavaro, Fabio
bio: Italian professional soccer coach and former player.

name: Kaká
bio: Brazilian-Italian soccer attacking midfielder who played from 2000 to 2017 with success at club and international level, becoming one of nine players to have won the FIFA World Cup, the UEFA Champions League and the Ballon d'Or.

name: Messi, Lionel
bio: Argentine expatriate soccer player, also known as Leo Messi, he plays as a forward for Ligue 1 club Paris Saint-Germain since 2021 and captains the Argentina national team.

name: Modrić, Luka
bio: Croatian soccer midfielder who plays for Spanish club Real Madrid and is the captain of the Croatia national team.

name: Benzema, Karim
bio: French international footballer born in Lyon to French nationals of Algerian descent.

name: Dembélé, Ousmane
bio: French footballer of Malian and Senegalese descent, playing as forward for FC Barcelona (formerly Borussia Dortmund) and for the French national team.

name: Maradona, Diego
bio: Argentine soccer champion who scored the winning goal in the final 1986 World Cup Game, when Argentina beat Mexico.

name: Pelé
bio: During his career, he became known as "The Black Pearl" (A Pérola Negra), "The King of Football" (O Rei do Futebol), "The King Pelé" (O Rei Pelé) or simply "The King" (O Rei).

name: Mbappé, Kylian
bio: Widely regarded as one of the best players in the world, he is known for his dribbling, speed, and finishing.

name: Lewandowski, Robert
bio: Polish football player, playing for the Bayern Munich and captaining his national team.

name: Krankl, Hans
bio: Austrian footballer, a prolific striker regarded by many as one of Austria's greatest players.

name: Friend, Donald
bio: Australian artist and diarist who lived much of his life overseas, in places as diverse as Nigeria (late 1930s, where he served as financial advisor to the Ogoga of Ikerre), Italy (several visits in the 1950s), Ceylon (now Sri Lanka; late 1950s – early 1960s), and Bali (from 1968 until his final return to Sydney in 1980).

name: Modugno, Domenico
bio: Italian singer and songwriter who took top prize at the 1958 San Remo music festival for the song "Nel Blu, Dipinto Di Blu," a song know as, "Volare," in the U.S.

name: Kennedy, James C.
bio: American billionaire, media executive and the current chair of Cox Enterprises, the conglomerate founded by his grandfather, James M. Cox.

name: Carpenter, A. J.
bio: American Rear Admiral in the United States Coast Guard.

name: Manheim, Camryn
bio: American actress known for her roles as attorney Ellenor Frutt on ABC's "The Practice," Delia Banks on CBS's "Ghost Whisperer," Gladys Presley in the 2005 miniseries "Elvis," and 'Control' on Person of Interest.

name: Bartlett, Heather
bio: American talent pageant competitor who was crowned Miss Vermont’s Outstanding Teen 2006 at age 16.

name: Puig, Manuel
bio: Argentine author.

name: Croccolo, Carlo
bio: Italian stage and screen actor, voice actor, director and screenwriter, who appeared in over 100 films since 1949.

name: Aldred, Sophie
bio: British entertainer, appearing in the TV series "Doctor Who" and co-host of a paranormal series "It's a Mystery," September 1996.

name: Bacharan, Nicole
bio: French-American historian and political scientist, famous for her books and TV appearances in France and the United States.

name: Maydaichevsky, Ivan
bio: French dancer.

name: Zutphen, Jan van
bio: Dutch trade unionist of the diamond workers' union and founder and chairman of sanatorium Zonnestraal ('Sunray' Sanatorium) in Hilversum, now on the Unesco World heritage list.

name: Otto, Elinor
bio: American woman, one of the original Rosie the Riveters, having built airplanes for 50 years.

name: Bonnell, Phillip Lee
bio: American noted family, the first son of actress Gale Storm and actor Lee Bonnel.

name: Morissette, Alanis
bio: Canadian-American musician, singer, songwriter and actress, known for her emotive mezzo-soprano voice and confessional songwriting.

name: Blackman, Charles
bio: Australian painter, noted for the Schoolgirl, Avonsleigh and Alice in Wonderland series of the 1950s.

name: Forrester, Eugene Priest
bio: American military, major general.

name: Ashbery, John
bio: American poet and professor, winner of the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry among other prestigious awards.

name: Richelot, Louis-Gustave
bio: French surgeon and gynaecologist whose main work appeared in the Union médicale, of which he was director, and in the Dictionnaire encyclopédique des Sciences médicales (Encyclopaedic Dictionary of Medical Sciences).

name: Trapp, Werner von
bio: Austrian-American singer from a noted family, as the second-oldest son of Georg von Trapp and his first wife, Agathe Whitehead von Trapp.

name: Arens, Skye
bio: American child actress onscreen from age one in the film Riding in Cars with Boys (2001), followed by such TV series as Bones (2005) and films like Wonder Woman (2009).

name: Uggams, Leslie
bio: American actress and singer with a career that began when she was five, making a TV debut at seven.

name: Modigliani, Jeanne
bio: French noted family, the daughter of famous painter Amedeo Modigliani.

name: Deluol, André
bio: French sculptor who died in Saint-Michel-en-l'Herm on 26 April 2003.

name: Pieters, Sven
bio: Belgian field and track champion, the holder of the Belgian record, which he improved at the Atlanta Olympics on 2 July 1996.

name: Gambineri, Annamaria
bio: Italian TV announcer since the beginning of Italian TV.

name: Christensen, Haaken
bio: Norwegian art historian, art collector, and gallery owner.

name: Reedy, George
bio: American government official, who served as the eighth White House Press Secretary under President Lyndon B. Johnson from 1964 to 1965.

name: Claxton, William
bio: American photographer and author, best known for his photography of jazz musicians including Chet Baker, and celebrities and models.

name: Tallarico, Taj Monroe
bio: American noted family, the second child and son of Teresa and Steven Tyler, lead singer of "Aerosmith."
